metadata.name: CSRD (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive)
metadata.description: The Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) is an EU directive that modernizes and strengthens sustainability reporting requirements for large companies and listed SMEs. It extends mandatory sustainability reporting to approximately 50,000 EU companies and requires reporting using European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS).
metadata.classification_reasoning: CSRD is a legally binding EU directive requiring mandatory sustainability reporting and third-party assurance for large EU companies and listed SMEs, with phased implementation from 2024-2028.
metadata.framework_purpose: To improve the consistency, comparability, and reliability of corporate sustainability information to enable investors and stakeholders to make informed decisions about sustainability risks and impacts.
metadata.framework_scope: Large EU public interest entities (PIEs) from 2024, other large companies from 2025, listed SMEs from 2026, and non-EU companies with significant EU business from 2028.
framework_objectives: Require limited assurance on sustainability disclosures
Align with EU Taxonomy and other EU sustainable finance legislation
metadata.target_audience: CFOs, Sustainability Officers, ESG Teams, Boards of Directors, Legal/Compliance Teams, Auditors, and all large EU companies
metadata.regulatory_authority: European Commission / European Financial Reporting Advisory Group (EFRAG)
adoption_approach: Assess scope and determine when CSRD applies
Prepare sustainability statement in management report
Obtain limited assurance from external auditor

controls.title: Double Materiality Assessment
controls.description: Companies must conduct a double materiality assessment to identify material sustainability topics covering both impact materiality and financial materiality.
controls.full_text: Conduct a double materiality assessment (DMA) covering: (1) Impact materiality — actual or potential significant impacts on people and environment (inward and outward), and (2) Financial materiality — sustainability-related risks and opportunities with significant financial effects. The DMA determines which ESRS topics are material and must be reported.
evidence_requirements: Materiality matrix covering impact and financial materiality

controls.title: Governance Structure and Sustainability Oversight
controls.description: Disclose the governance bodies and functions responsible for sustainability, including their roles in oversight, decision-making, and accountability.
controls.full_text: Disclose the roles of governance bodies (board, supervisory board, management) in sustainability oversight. Include how sustainability is integrated into governance including sustainability-related competencies, information flows to governance bodies, and integration of sustainability in remuneration policies. Disclose the sustainability statement preparation process.

controls.title: Business Model and Value Chain Sustainability
controls.description: Disclose the company's business model, value chain, and how sustainability considerations are integrated into strategy.
controls.full_text: Describe the company's business model and value chain including: main business activities, markets, products, customers, and supply chain. Describe how sustainability risks and opportunities influence business model and strategy. Disclose the resilience of strategy to sustainability risks and how the company plans to adjust strategy for transition.
evidence_requirements: Business model and value chain description

controls.title: Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to climate change, including GHG emissions, energy consumption, and climate transition plan.
controls.full_text: Report on: (1) Climate change governance, (2) Climate strategy and transition plan, (3) Targets for GHG reduction (aligned with Paris Agreement), (4) Policies and actions for climate change, (5) Metrics: Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG emissions, energy from fossil and renewable sources, and climate risk financial impacts.

controls.title: Pollution Prevention and Control
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to pollution of air, water, soil, and substances of concern.
controls.full_text: Report on pollution of air (emissions to air), water (discharges), soil (contamination), substances of concern, and microplastics. Disclose: pollution governance, targets for pollution reduction, prevention and control policies, actions taken, and metrics for pollutant releases and substances of very high concern (SVHCs).

controls.original_reference: ESRS E3 - Water and Marine Resources
controls.title: Water and Marine Resource Use and Management
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to water and marine resources.
controls.full_text: Report on: water withdrawal, consumption, and discharge; water efficiency targets; water stress area exposures; marine resource use; and policies for water stewardship. Disclose metrics including total water consumption, water recycled, and proportion of operations in water-stressed areas.

controls.title: Biodiversity and Ecosystems Impact Management
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to biodiversity and ecosystems.
controls.full_text: Report on biodiversity impacts including land use change, freshwater and marine ecosystem impacts, direct exploitation, invasive species, and pollution. Disclose sites in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas. Report on biodiversity targets and nature-positive commitments. Align with the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework.

controls.original_reference: ESRS E5 - Resource Use and Circular Economy
controls.title: Resource Use and Circular Economy
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to resource use and transition to circular economy.
controls.full_text: Report on: resource use (materials, water, energy); waste generation and management; circularity strategies (design for longevity, repair, reuse, recycling); and targets for waste reduction and circular economy transition. Disclose metrics including total waste, waste diverted, recycled content, and circular economy revenue.

controls.title: Own Workforce Working Conditions and Human Rights
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to its own workforce, including working conditions, health and safety, and diversity.
controls.full_text: Report on own workforce characteristics (headcount, gender split, contract type, by region), working conditions (wages, working hours, OH&S), equal treatment and opportunities (gender pay gap, diversity metrics), and other work-related rights. Disclose policies, targets, and metrics for workforce topics.

controls.original_reference: ESRS S2 - Workers in Value Chain
controls.title: Workers in the Value Chain
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to workers in its value chain (suppliers and customers).
controls.full_text: Report on working conditions and human rights in the supply chain. Conduct supply chain human rights due diligence. Identify salient human rights issues in supply chain. Disclose policies for responsible sourcing, supplier audits, and remediation of supply chain violations.
evidence_requirements: Supply chain human rights due diligence

controls.title: Affected Communities
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to affected communities, including indigenous peoples.
controls.full_text: Report on economic, social, and cultural impacts on communities affected by operations. Address land rights, indigenous peoples' rights, human rights defenders, and community grievances. Disclose community engagement processes and free, prior, and informed consent (FPIC) where applicable.

controls.title: Consumers and End-Users
controls.description: Disclose the company's material impacts, risks, and opportunities related to consumers and end-users of its products and services.
controls.full_text: Report on impacts on consumers and end-users including product safety, customer privacy, responsible marketing, access to products and services, and responsible business practices. Disclose customer satisfaction metrics, product safety incidents, and actions to enhance responsible consumption.

controls.title: Business Conduct and Anti-Corruption
controls.description: Disclose the company's business conduct practices including anti-corruption, whistleblowing, responsible lobbying, supplier relationships, and payment practices.
controls.full_text: Report on: corporate culture and business conduct, management of relationships with suppliers and customers, prevention and detection of corruption and bribery, anti-competitive behavior, political engagement, and payment practices. Disclose metrics including confirmed incidents of corruption, fines for non-compliance, and payment terms for suppliers.

controls.title: Limited Assurance on Sustainability Reporting
controls.description: CSRD requires limited assurance from a statutory auditor or accredited third party on the sustainability statement.
controls.full_text: Obtain limited assurance from the statutory auditor or an accredited independent assurance provider on the sustainability statement. Assurance must cover compliance with ESRS, double materiality process, and compliance with EU Taxonomy. Reasonable assurance (higher standard) will be required in later years. Report auditor's conclusion in the sustainability statement.

controls.title: EU Taxonomy-Aligned Activity Disclosure
controls.description: CSRD requires disclosure of EU Taxonomy-eligible and aligned activities as part of the sustainability statement.
controls.full_text: Disclose the proportion of turnover, CapEx, and OpEx from EU Taxonomy-aligned activities. Assess activities against technical screening criteria (substantial contribution, do no significant harm, and minimum social safeguards). Report taxonomy-eligible activities as a first step and then aligned activities after detailed assessment.

controls.title: Digital Sustainability Reporting
controls.description: CSRD requires sustainability information to be tagged in XBRL format as part of the European Single Electronic Format (ESEF).
controls.full_text: Tag sustainability disclosures in compliance with ESEF requirements using the ESRS XBRL taxonomy. The XBRL tags enable machine-readable access to sustainability data for investors and regulators. Report the sustainability statement as part of the ESEF-compliant management report.

controls.title: CSRD Applicability and Value Chain Reporting
controls.description: Determine CSRD applicability, reporting boundary, and value chain reporting requirements.
controls.full_text: Determine if the company meets CSRD thresholds (large company: 250+ employees, €40M+ turnover, €20M+ balance sheet; listed SME or non-EU company with significant EU business). Define the sustainability reporting boundary including subsidiaries. Report material impacts, risks, and opportunities across the value chain (upstream suppliers, own operations, downstream customers).

controls.title: Sustainability Targets and Forward-Looking Disclosures
controls.description: CSRD requires disclosure of measurable sustainability targets and time-bound commitments aligned with business strategy.
controls.full_text: Set and disclose measurable sustainability targets for material topics with defined timelines. Targets must be aligned with business strategy and EU sustainability objectives. Report progress against targets with quantitative metrics. Address climate targets in alignment with Paris Agreement and EU Green Deal objectives.
evidence_requirements: Sustainability targets for each material topic
Target alignment with EU sustainability policy
